title: Sarah Margon Joins CNAS as Adjunct Senior Fellow
gist: CNAS announced that Sarah Margon, a veteran U.S. foreign-policy and human-rights strategist (Human Rights Watch, Center for American Progress, Soros Fund Management/Open Society Foundations, and a 2021 Biden nominee for assistant secretary of state for democracy, human rights, and labor), has joined the think tank as an adjunct senior fellow, with CEO Richard Fontaine framing the hire as strengthening CNAS's work at a moment when democracy and human rights are central to American security.

insights.claim: CNAS announced on June 26, 2026 that Sarah Margon has joined the think tank as an adjunct senior fellow, following more than two decades working at the intersection of U.S. foreign policy, philanthropy, civil society, and the private sector.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Signals CNAS is deepening its bench on democracy- and human-rights-focused foreign policy talent, relevant for tracking the think tank's future research agenda and its pipeline of personnel into future administrations.
insights.locator: The Center for a New American Security (CNAS) today announced

insights.claim: CNAS CEO Richard Fontaine framed Margon's hire as strengthening the think tank's work at a moment when democracy and human-rights issues are 'central to American security,' explicitly tying human-rights policy to national-security strategy.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Indicates CNAS leadership is positioning democracy and human-rights issues as core to its national-security research agenda rather than peripheral, useful context for interpreting future CNAS publications and advocacy.
insights.quote: these issues are central to American security
insights.locator: "Sarah has devoted her career to making American foreign policy..." said Richard Fontaine

insights.claim: Margon founded and runs Windsong Advisory, a strategic consulting firm, and most recently worked at Soros Fund Management, which manages the Open Society Foundations' endowment, after earlier serving as Open Society's director of U.S. foreign policy.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Ties CNAS's new fellow into the Soros/Open Society philanthropic network, useful context for mapping funding and influence relationships around the think tank's human-rights-adjacent work.
insights.locator: Margon is the founder and principal of Windsong Advisory

insights.claim: Margon's earlier career spans Washington director at Human Rights Watch, associate director for sustainable security at the Center for American Progress, senior foreign policy advisor to Senator Russ Feingold, and staff director for the Senate Foreign Relations Subcommittee on African Affairs; in 2021 President Biden nominated her as assistant secretary of state for democracy, human rights, and labor.
insights.speaker: CNAS
entities: Senate Foreign Relations Subcommittee on African Affairs
insights.so_what: Her deep Democratic-aligned human-rights network and an unconfirmed 2021 Biden-era nomination signal CNAS is recruiting talent aligned with a rights-based foreign-policy wing, relevant for anticipating CNAS's positioning across administrations.
insights.locator: Earlier in her career, Margon was Washington director
